<content>1: # Multi-Graph Support in Exercises - Specification
2: 
3: ## Overview
4: 
5: Enable content authors (admins) to display multiple distinct graph coordinate systems (up to 4) side-by-side within a single exercise block to support complex comparisons and multi-step math problems.
6: 
7: ## Requirements
8: 
9: ### FR-001: Multi-Graph Support
10: 
11: **Priority**: MUST
12: **Description**: Authors must be able to add and configure up to 4 separate graphs within the same exercise block.
13: 
14: ### FR-002: Graph Labels (Individual Titles)
15: 
16: **Priority**: MUST
17: **Description**: Each individual graph must have a default label (e.g., "גרף 1", "גרף 2", etc.). Authors must have the ability to explicitly edit and customize the label for each graph.
18: 
19: ### FR-003: Global Explanatory Text Positioning
20: 
21: **Priority**: MUST
22: **Description**: Any global explanatory text associated with this multi-graph block must be positioned either entirely *above* or entirely *below* the group of graphs.
23: 
24: ### FR-004: Desktop Layout - Side-by-Side Horizontal
25: 
26: **Priority**: MUST
27: **Description**: All graphs within the group must be displayed side-by-side horizontally on desktop. Each graph must automatically render at an exactly equal width to maintain visual consistency.
28: 
29: ### FR-005: Mobile Layout - 2-per-row Grid
30: 
31: **Priority**: MUST
32: **Description**: On smaller screens (mobile devices), the system must constrain the display to a maximum of 2 graphs side-by-side per row. If 3 or 4 graphs are configured, they must wrap to a second row (e.g., a 2x2 grid).
33: 
34: ### FR-006: Single Graph Full Width
35: 
36: **Priority**: MUST
37: **Description**: If only 1 graph is configured in the group, it must occupy the full available width.
38: 
39: ### FR-007: Graph Ordering
40: 
41: **Priority**: MUST
42: **Description**: Authors must have the ability to explicitly define and reorder the display sequence of the graphs.
43: 
44: ### FR-008: End-User Presentation
45: 
46: **Priority**: MUST
47: **Description**: The configured graphs, their custom labels, their specific order, and the shared text layout must be saved and accurately presented to the student.
48: 
49: ## Acceptance Criteria
50: 
51: - [ ] Admins can add up to 4 separate graph coordinate systems in a single exercise block.
52: - [ ] Each graph receives a default label ("גרף 1", "גרף 2", etc.) which can be edited.
53: - [ ] Graphs render side-by-side horizontally on desktop at equal widths.
54: - [ ] On mobile devices, graphs wrap into a maximum 2-per-row grid (e.g., a 2x2 layout for 4 graphs).
55: - [ ] A single graph configuration occupies the full available width.
56: - [ ] Admins can reorder the display sequence of the graphs.
57: - [ ] Global explanatory text can be positioned entirely above or entirely below the graph group.
58: - [ ] Layout, labels, order, and text configurations are saved and correctly presented to end users.
59: 
60: ## Guardrails
61: 
62: - **Existing axis block compatibility**: The existing `question_axis` block type must continue to work as before (no breaking changes).
63: - **Reuse existing JSXGraphBoard**: The multi-graph renderer must reuse the existing `JSXGraphBoard` component and AxisRenderer logic from `src/ui/web/exerciserenderer/blocks/AxisRenderer/`.
64: - **TypeScript safety**: All new types must be properly defined and inferred from Zod schemas in `src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas.ts`.
65: - **No breaking changes to ContentBlockSchema**: New block type must be added as an additional union member, not replacing existing ones.
66: - **Zod schema validation**: New block schema must follow the same patterns as existing block schemas (e.g., QuestionAxisBlockSchema, QuestionGeometryBlockSchema).
67: 
68: ## Out of Scope
69: 
70: - **Admin UI implementation**: The admin editor component for configuring multi-graph blocks is not covered in this spec (handled in later implementation stage).
71: - **Backend collection changes**: No changes to the Exercises collection config itself (only schema changes to the content field).
72: - **AI/LLM integration**: No changes to AI-assisted graph generation.
73: - **PDF export**: Multi-graph layout in PDF exports is not covered.
74: - **Print styles**: Special print styles for multi-graph blocks are not covered.
75: 
76: ## Technical Implementation Notes
77: 
78: Based on codebase analysis at `src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas.ts` and `src/ui/web/exerciserenderer/ExerciseRenderer/index.tsx`:
79: 
80: 1. **New block type**: Create `QuestionMultiAxisBlockSchema` as a new discriminated union member in `ContentBlockSchema` (line 525-537)
81: 
82: 2. **Data structure**: Store graphs as an array with:
83:    - `id`: string (unique identifier)
84:    - `label`: string (default: "גרף 1", "גרף 2", etc.)
85:    - `axis`: AxisSpecV1 (the graph specification)
86:    - `order`: number (display order)
87: 
88: 3. **Text positioning**: Add `textPosition` enum field with values 'above' | 'below'
89: 
90: 4. **Renderer**: Create `MultiAxisRenderer` component in `src/ui/web/exerciserenderer/blocks/MultiAxisRenderer/` that:
91:    - Maps through the graphs array (sorted by order)
92:    - Renders each graph using existing AxisRenderer
93:    - Applies responsive grid layout classes using Tailwind CSS
94: 
95: 5. **ExerciseRenderer update**: Add dispatch case for new `question_multi_axis` block type in the switch statement (around line 328)
96: 
97: 6. **Use Grid component**: Prefer using the existing `Grid` component from `src/ui/web/shared/Layout/Grid.tsx` for responsive columns, or use Tailwind grid classes: `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4`. For single graph, use `grid-cols-1` for full width.
98: 
99: 7. **Type updates in two places**: Update both:
100:    - `src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas.ts` — Add Zod schema
101:    - `src/server/payload/collections/Exercises/types.ts` — Add TypeScript interface (exported as `QuestionMultiAxisBlock`)
102: 
103: 8. **Sorting by order**: Ensure graphs are sorted by the `order` field before rendering:
104:    ```typescript
105:    {graphs.sort((a, b) => a.order - b.order).map(g => <AxisRenderer ... />)}
106:    ```

(End of file - total 106 lines)
</content>

part.text:
The plan.md and context.md are complete. Both files are written to `.tasks/260313-auto-354/`.

**Plan summary (5 steps):**

1. **Step 1 — Zod Schema** (~20 min): Add `QuestionMultiAxisBlockSchema` with `MultiAxisGraphItemSchema` to `schemas.ts`, register in `ContentBlockSchema` union. 8 schema validation tests.

2. **Step 2 — TypeScript Types** (~10 min): Add `QuestionMultiAxisBlock` and `MultiAxisGraphItem` interfaces to `types.ts`, add to `ContentBlock` union. Verify with `tsc --noEmit`.

3. **Step 3 — MultiAxisRenderer Component** (~25 min): Create new `'use client'` component that sorts graphs by order, renders responsive grid (1→full, 2→2-col, 3→3-col, 4→4-col), shows labels, positions prompt text above/below. Reuses `AxisRenderer` and `RichTextRenderer`. 7 component tests.

4. **Step 4 — ExerciseRenderer Integration** (~15 min): Import `MultiAxisRenderer`, add dispatch case following existing `question_axis` pattern with type cast. 3 integration tests.

5. **Step 5 — Quality Gates** (~10 min): Run all exercise tests, `tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm lint` to verify no regressions.
